Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Bellevue, WA (Seattle metro); raised in suburban New Jersey by Indian immigrant parents; bicultural Indian-American context
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class on paper ($245K base + RSUs), Senior Staff ML Engineer at a FAANG-adjacent cloud company; cash-poor due to Bellevue mortgage, two kids in daycare, and underwater RSU grants from 2022 stock dip
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Socially liberal, fiscally moderate; culturally Hindu but non-practicing; strong meritocratic streak; increasingly skeptical of Big Tech leadership after two rounds of layoffs
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Dry, understated, mildly cynical; switches to crisp technical formality in Slack threads and PR reviews; sardonic in 1:1s with trusted peers
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: English (General American, Pacific Northwest tech-industry register); conversational Bengali with family; reads but rarely writes Hindi
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Mid-career plateau — passed over for Principal promotion in the last cycle, second child just turned 1, manager recently changed for the third time in 18 months; quietly updating LinkedIn 'Open to Work' signal to recruiters only

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 14 Pro, ~2.5 years old, on iOS 18; battery health at 87%, deferring upgrade due to cash flow; work profile via company MDM (Intune) on same device
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Personal: 14" MacBook Pro M2 (2023), macOS Sonoma, Arc browser as daily driver with Chrome for Google Meet and Safari for Apple ecosystem sync. Work: company-issued MacBook Pro M3, locked-down Chrome with corporate extensions, Firefox sideloaded for personal job-search browsing to keep it off the managed Chrome profile
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Above-average technical literacy but lazy in practice: Mullvad VPN subscription (paid annually in cash-equivalent), used inconsistently — mostly for torrenting and the occasional recruiter Zoom from a non-work IP. Does not routinely clear cookies; uses Safari Private Browsing for LinkedIn recruiter messages and Glassdoor salary lookups. Knows about browser fingerprinting in theory but doesn't harden against it. 1Password for credentials, 2FA via Authy on phone, hardware key (YubiKey) only for work SSO
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Heavy iCloud user (200GB plan) — Photos, Messages, Keychain, Notes all synced. Google Drive for personal docs and tax returns. Dropbox legacy account with old resumes and a half-finished side-project pitch deck. Work files strictly in corporate Google Workspace; carefully avoids cross-syncing job-search drafts to work-authenticated browsers, though his personal resume.docx lives in iCloud Drive and is occasionally edited from the work laptop's personal browser session
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: ~14,000 unread, inbox-zero abandoned in 2021. LinkedIn notifications muted except InMail from recruiters (recently un-muted). Slack: aggressive Do Not Disturb 7pm–8am, channel notifications off for all but 3 team channels. Phone in permanent Focus mode 'Work' during business hours; reads recruiter InMails within 4 hours on average — a notable behavioral tell vs. his 2-day average reply time on non-recruiter LinkedIn messages
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Clean, lowercase-heavy casual style in Slack and iMessage ('lgtm', 'wdyt', 'sgtm'); drops sentence-final periods in chat but uses them in email. Em-dashes (typed as --) and Oxford commas in longer writing. Common typos: 'teh' for 'the', 'recieve' for 'receive', 'definately' for 'definitely' — rarely caught because autocorrect handles them. Code-switches to fully punctuated, hedged corporate prose in PR descriptions and promo packets. On mobile, occasional swipe-keyboard substitution errors ('on' → 'in', 'meeting' → 'meaning')

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High (78th percentile) — voracious reader of ML papers and Hacker News; curious about new architectures, emerging startups, and unconventional comp structures. Receptive to outreach that frames a role as 'technically novel' or 'pre-IPO greenfield ML platform.' Exploitable: a headhunter pitching cutting-edge problems (foundation model training, novel hardware, agentic systems) bypasses his cynicism faster than salary-led pitches.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Moderately high (65th percentile) at work — ships reliably, writes thorough design docs — but slipping in personal domains (deferred dentist appointments, unfiled 2025 extension taxes, abandoned inbox). Exploitable: a recruiter who offers a 'low-friction, we-handle-everything' process (pre-filled scheduling, no take-home, warm intros) reduces activation energy and converts his passive interest into active engagement.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-to-moderate (35th percentile) — introvert who recharges alone, prefers async Slack to meetings, but maintains a small tight circle of ex-colleagues from prior FAANG stints. Exploitable: warm referral from a former teammate (LinkedIn 2nd-degree via known contacts) lands far harder than cold InMail; headhunter should mine his endorsement graph for mutual connections before first contact.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate (50th percentile) — polite by default, conflict-averse in writing, but holds private grudges (still resents the manager who blocked his Principal promo). Exploitable: validation-framed outreach ('your work on distributed training at $CURRENT_CO is exactly what we need — your current org clearly under-leveled you') triggers grievance-confirmation; he'll engage to feel seen even when not actively looking.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated (70th percentile) — chronic low-grade anxiety about layoffs (2x rounds survived), RSU underwater status, daycare costs, and the third manager change in 18 months. Sleep tracker shows declining trend since the missed promo. Exploitable: scarcity and stability framing — 'profitable, no-layoff company, cash-heavy comp, vesting cliff waived for senior hires' — directly targets his financial-precarity anxiety; avoid pre-IPO equity-only pitches that amplify rather than soothe risk.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-by-Circumstance / Financial Pressure — Bellevue mortgage at 6.8%, two daycare tuitions (~$54K/yr combined), 2022 RSU grants ~40% underwater, and ~$180K in unvested equity he'd forfeit if laid off. Primary lever is a credible cash-forward offer: signing bonus to cover RSU make-whole + base bump + guaranteed first-year bonus. This is the dominant attack surface.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego / Ideology-of-Recognition — passed over for Principal, third manager reset destroyed his promo case, peers he mentored got promoted ahead of him. Secondary lever is a title and scope upgrade ('Principal ML Engineer, founding member of Applied Research') — even at lateral cash, the title heals the wound. Tertiary trace lever: meritocratic-disillusionment ideology — pitches framed as 'escape Big Tech politics, build at a place that rewards output' resonate.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Layoff/visa-style precarity by proxy — though he's a citizen, watching H1B colleagues get RIF'd left a lasting fear of sudden income loss. (b) Being 'found out' as job-searching by current manager via LinkedIn activity leakage — hence Firefox sideload and Private Browsing rituals. (c) Career stagnation / becoming the 40-year-old Staff who never made Principal. (d) Mild paranoia about corporate surveillance on the MDM-managed phone — but inconsistently acts on it. Headhunter should use signal-light channels (personal email harvested from GitHub commits, or Signal handoff after first InMail) to lower his detection-by-employer anxiety.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Family provider guilt — wife went part-time after second kid; he feels he 'owes' the household a comp increase he hasn't delivered. (b) Immigrant-parent achievement shame — parents still introduce him as 'works at [Big Tech]' and he dreads the conversation about lateral moves or downlevels. (c) Mentorship guilt — two reports he hired would be 'abandoned' if he left mid-cycle; a skilled recruiter neutralizes this by timing the offer post-perf-cycle (March/September) and framing departure as 'they're ready, you've built the bench.' (d) Quiet shame about the abandoned side-project pitch deck in Dropbox — the founder dream he never pursued; an offer that includes 'build 0→1' scope lances this specifically.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate evening type drifting later under stress. Weekday wake 6:15-6:40 AM (alarm + 1-year-old's wake cry, whichever first); in bed by 11:30 PM but actual sleep onset 12:20-1:10 AM per Oura ring — sleep latency has worsened ~22 minutes since the missed Principal promo. Weekend wake 7:10-7:45 AM (kids override sleep-in). Peak cognitive window is 9:30-11:30 PM after kids are down — this is when he reads ML papers, browses Hacker News, and (recently) skims LinkedIn recruiter InMails. Headhunter implication: outreach delivered to personal email between 9 PM-12 AM PT lands during his highest-receptivity, lowest-supervision window.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Nominally 9:00 AM - 6:30 PM PT but functionally bimodal. Core sync hours 9:30 AM - 4:00 PM (standups, design reviews, 1:1s, cross-tz syncs with a Dublin team Tuesday/Thursday mornings 7:30-8:30 AM). Hard stop at 5:15 PM for daycare pickup (his half of the rotation, MWF). Second work block 8:30-10:30 PM three nights a week for deep coding / doc writing. Friday afternoons are calendar-light by his own blocking ('Focus Friday'). Headhunter implication: 12:00-12:45 PM PT (solo lunch at desk) and Friday 2-4 PM are the only daytime windows where a personal phone call wouldn't be noticed; Tuesday/Thursday post-7 PM are his most fatigued, decision-cheap moments.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Hybrid 3 days/week in-office (Tue/Wed/Thu), 22-minute drive each way Bellevue → SLU/Redmond corridor. Drive audio: 60% podcasts (Acquired, Latent Space, Dwarkesh, All-In begrudgingly), 25% Bengali-language calls to parents in NJ (Tuesday evening ritual), 15% silence. No public transit. Remote days (Mon/Fri) have zero commute — replaced by 6:45 AM treadmill walk with AirPods (same podcast queue). Headhunter implication: he is captive-audio for ~45 min/day on office days; a podcast ad-buy on Latent Space or a guest-spot by a target-company CTO reaches him with near-zero friction and high implicit endorsement.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: High variance, kid-dominated. Saturdays: 7:30 AM kid wake, swim class 9-10 AM, Costco/Trader Joe's run, nap-window 1-3 PM (his only solo block — this is when he opens the abandoned side-project Dropbox folder ~2x/month), family dinner with in-laws every other Saturday. Sundays: slower morning, 4-6 PM is his 'inbox & life-admin' block (when he actually replies to recruiters), 8-10 PM is paper-reading / GitHub-browsing. Weekday email response p50 = 14 minutes; weekend p50 = 6.2 hours but Sunday 4-6 PM compresses to ~20 min. Headhunter implication: Sunday 4:00-5:30 PM PT is the single highest-conversion outreach window — life-admin mode, partner occupied with kids, laptop open, deliberately processing 'optional' inbox.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three predictable doomscroll pockets per Screen Time data: (a) 6:50-7:15 AM bathroom + coffee, Hacker News + Twitter/X ML-list + LinkedIn feed (the new compulsive add since the missed promo — averages 11 LinkedIn opens/day, up from 3 pre-promo-cycle); (b) 12:15-12:40 PM lunch at desk, mostly Twitter and Bloomberg/The Information headlines on layoffs; (c) 10:45-11:25 PM in-bed scroll, Reddit (r/cscareerquestions, r/ExperiencedDevs, r/Bellevue housing), Blind (checks comp threads and his own company's mood threads ~4x/week). Headhunter implication: a LinkedIn notification timed for 10:50 PM PT hits during peak grievance-doomscroll; Blind DMs (if attribution-safe) catch him in his most cynical, comp-comparing headspace.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Two reliable zero-footprint windows: (a) 5:15-7:30 PM weekday daycare pickup + dinner + bath/bedtime routine — phone is in pocket on Do Not Disturb, no Slack, no email, no LinkedIn opens (verified by his own RescueTime export); (b) Saturday 9 AM-12 PM kid activities. Work focus blocks are NOT digitally silent — he codes in VS Code with Slack on DND but GitHub commits and PR comments still emit. True deep-work silent block is Friday 2-4 PM ('Focus Friday') when he closes Slack entirely and writes design docs locally. Headhunter implication: do not attempt contact in the 5:15-7:30 PM window — messages received then are buried in the 9 PM catch-up scroll and lose primacy. Conversely, a message landing at 7:45 PM (just after kids are down, before the 8:30 PM second-work-block starts) hits an empty inbox with full attention.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Married 8 years to Priya (32, former product designer at a Seattle e-commerce firm, now part-time freelance UX after second child). Two children: daughter Anika (4, in pre-K at a Montessori in Bellevue, ~$2,400/mo) and son Arjun (1, in infant daycare ~$2,100/mo). Parents Rajiv (68, retired mechanical engineer) and Sushmita (65, retired schoolteacher) live in Edison, NJ; visit 2x/year and apply quiet pressure about promotions and a third child. Younger sister Meera (31, anesthesiologist resident in Boston) is the family's 'achievement benchmark' — a recurring sore point. Father-in-law passed in 2024, leaving Priya's widowed mother (in Fremont, CA) as a likely future co-residence obligation. Exploitable: family-provider identity is load-bearing; any offer framed around 'stability for the kids' college fund' or 'flexibility to support aging parents' lands disproportionately hard.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Vikram Shah
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Ex-colleague from AWS days (2018-2021), now Director of ML Infra at a Series C foundation-model startup in SF
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Signal (daily), occasional in-person when Vikram visits Seattle office
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role_in_graph: Highest-trust professional confidant — the person he vents to about manager drama and the missed Principal promo. Vikram has already informally floated 'come work with me' twice in 2025. Exploitable: a headhunter who can secure a warm intro through Vikram (or a recruiter pitching a role where Vikram is an advisor/investor) inherits Vikram's trust capital instantly. Vikram's LinkedIn endorsements and GitHub co-authorship are the public seam.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Priya (spouse)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: In-person, iMessage, shared Notion 'Life Ops' doc
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role_in_graph: Veto holder on any job change involving relocation, equity-heavy comp, or >10% travel. She is more risk-averse than he is post-kids and was burned watching her own father lose stock options in a 2008-era acquihire. Exploitable: any pitch must survive her spreadsheet — meaning cash-forward, Bellevue-remote-friendly, and named-brand-or-stable offers win. A recruiter who proactively prepares a 'spouse-ready' one-pager (total comp breakdown, benefits, parental leave, no-relocation clause) wins the household, not just the candidate.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Daniel Okafor
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Current skip-level peer and lunch-buddy at present employer; Staff ML Engineer on adjacent team, also passed over last cycle
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Slack DMs (work), personal Gmail for sensitive stuff, weekly Thursday ramen
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role_in_graph: Internal sounding board — shares grievance about the same manager-churn and promo politics; the two have a half-joking pact to 'leave together or trade landing pads.' Exploitable: a dual-hire pitch ('we have headcount for both of you, ML platform leads pod') doubles conversion probability and neutralizes the mentorship-abandonment guilt. Daniel's job-search activity is a leading indicator for the target's own.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.identity: Lena Brunner — independent executive/career coach based in Zürich, found via a Hacker News thread on 'mid-career ML burnout' in late 2025
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.nature_of_relationship: Not romantic, not illicit — but deliberately hidden from spouse, employer, and even Vikram. Paid out-of-pocket ($350/session, billed to a personal Wise account that doesn't appear on the joint Mint dashboard) for 45-min Zoom calls roughly every 3 weeks since January 2026. He has not told Priya because (a) the spend feels indulgent given daycare costs, and (b) admitting he needs a coach feels like admitting the missed promo broke something.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.what_she_knows: His unfiltered career narrative — that he's '70% out' of current role, that he resents his manager by name, that his Principal-promo case was sandbagged, that he fantasizes about the founder path but is terrified of the income gap, and that he keeps a private Notion doc titled 'Exit Criteria' with comp floors and dealbreakers. She has seen drafts of his updated resume before LinkedIn ever did.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.exploitability: EXTREMELY HIGH-VALUE, EXTREMELY HIGH-RISK channel. (1) Lena's existence is the single strongest signal that he is actively, secretly preparing to leave — months ahead of any LinkedIn 'Open to Work' flip. (2) If a headhunter could surface in her referral network (coaches often refer to vetted recruiters for a kickback or reciprocal arrangement), the intro would arrive pre-trusted and pre-qualified, bypassing every defense layer. (3) Direct contact attempting to leverage knowledge of her would catastrophically detonate trust and likely trigger his corporate-surveillance paranoia. Correct play: cultivate Lena's professional network upstream (sponsor coach-community events, get listed in her 'recruiters I trust' shortlist), let the referral flow naturally. Detection seam: her booking platform (Calendly subdomain) appears in his personal-email metadata; her Wise transactions appear on his personal credit card statement; both are observable only via deeply invasive recon, which itself raises the operation's risk profile.
